code:
struct Player {
    pos           // current physical translation (Vec2)
    prev          // previous physical translation (for interpolation)
    vel           // Vec2
    half          // half-extents (Vec2)
    aim           // Vec2 unit vector
    speed_mult    // 1.0 normal, run_multiplier, sneak_multiplier
    hp            // hit points — attacks subtract, respawn on 0
    iframes       // invulnerability timer (seconds) after being hit
    stamina       // current stamina (0..stamina_max), drained by sprinting
}

struct Tuning {
    max_speed
    accel
    friction
    light_range
    light_half_angle_deg
    light_intensity
    ambient
    camera_smoothing
    run_multiplier
    sneak_multiplier
    stamina_max      // max stamina pool
    stamina_drain    // stamina drained per second while sprinting
    stamina_regen    // stamina recovered per second while not sprinting
}

use beagle.reflect as reflect
reflect/fields(g/Player)

use beagle.reflect as reflect
reflect/fields(g/Player)
g/default_tuning()

struct UiBuffers {
    max_speed
    accel
    friction
    light_range
    light_half_angle_deg
    light_intensity
    ambient
    camera_smoothing
    run_mult
    sneak_mult
    stamina_max
    stamina_drain
    stamina_regen
}

fn alloc_ui_buffers(t) {
    UiBuffers {
        max_speed:            ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.max_speed),
        accel:                ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.accel),
        friction:             ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.friction),
        light_range:          ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.light_range),
        light_half_angle_deg: ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.light_half_angle_deg),
        light_intensity:      ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.light_intensity),
        ambient:              ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.ambient),
        camera_smoothing:     ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.camera_smoothing),
        run_mult:             ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.run_multiplier),
        sneak_mult:           ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.sneak_multiplier),
        stamina_max:          ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.stamina_max),
        stamina_drain:        ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.stamina_drain),
        stamina_regen:        ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.stamina_regen)
    }
}

fn read_tuning_from_ui(bufs) {
    g/Tuning {
        max_speed:            ffi/cell-get(bufs.max_speed),
        accel:                ffi/cell-get(bufs.accel),
        friction:             ffi/cell-get(bufs.friction),
        light_range:          ffi/cell-get(bufs.light_range),
        light_half_angle_deg: ffi/cell-get(bufs.light_half_angle_deg),
        light_intensity:      ffi/cell-get(bufs.light_intensity),
        ambient:              ffi/cell-get(bufs.ambient),
        camera_smoothing:     ffi/cell-get(bufs.camera_smoothing),
        run_multiplier:       ffi/cell-get(bufs.run_mult),
        sneak_multiplier:     ffi/cell-get(bufs.sneak_mult),
        stamina_max:          ffi/cell-get(bufs.stamina_max),
        stamina_drain:        ffi/cell-get(bufs.stamina_drain),
        stamina_regen:        ffi/cell-get(bufs.stamina_regen)
    }
}

let p = g/Player { pos: v/ZERO, prev: v/ZERO, vel: v/ZERO, half: v/make(14.0, 14.0), aim: v/UNIT_X, speed_mult: 1.0, hp: 3.0, iframes: 0.0, stamina: 100.0 }
p.stamina

    accel

    light_half_angle_deg

    ambient

struct Player {
    pos           // current physical translation (Vec2)
    prev          // previous physical translation (for interpolation)
    vel           // Vec2
    half          // half-extents (Vec2)
    aim           // Vec2 unit vector
    speed_mult    // 1.0 normal, run_multiplier, sneak_multiplier
    stamina       // current stamina (0..stamina_max)
    hp            // hit points — attacks subtract, respawn on 0
    iframes       // invulnerability timer (seconds) after being hit
}

    UiBuffers {

        light_intensity:      ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.light_intensity),
        ambient:              ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.ambient),
        camera_smoothing:     ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.camera_smoothing),
        run_mult:             ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.run_multiplier),
        sneak_mult:           ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.sneak_multiplier),
        stamina_max:          ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.stamina_max),
        stamina_drain:        ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.stamina_sprint_drain),
        stamina_regen:        ffi/cell(ffi/Type.F32, t.stamina_regen)
    }
}

fn read_tuning_from_ui(bufs) {
    g/Tuning {
        max_speed:            ffi/cell-get(bufs.max_speed),
        accel:                ffi/cell-get(bufs.accel),
        friction:             ffi/cell-get(bufs.friction),
        light_range:          ffi/cell-get(bufs.light_range),
        light_half_angle_deg: ffi/cell-get(bufs.light_half_angle_deg),
        light_intensity:      ffi/cell-get(bufs.light_intensity),
        ambient:              ffi/cell-get(bufs.ambient),
        camera_smoothing:     ffi/cell-get(bufs.camera_smoothing),
        run_multiplier:       ffi/cell-get(bufs.run_mult),
        sneak_multiplier:     ffi/cell-get(bufs.sneak_mult),
        stamina_max:          ffi/cell-get(bufs.stamina_max),
        stamina_sprint_drain: ffi/cell-get(bufs.stamina_drain),
        stamina_regen:        ffi/cell-get(bufs.stamina_regen)
    }
}

fn draw_editor_panel(world, ui_bufs) {
    let panel_x = to-float(r/screen_width()) - 260.0
    r/panel(panel_x, 0.0, 260.0, to-float(r/screen_height()), "Editor")

    r/label(panel_x + 10.0, 30.0, 240.0, 20.0, world.editor.status_text)

    // Map nav — which map are we editing? Prev/Next cycles MAP_NAMES and
    // reloads from disk (or hardcoded fallback).
    r/label(panel_x + 10.0, 54.0, 240.0, 18.0, "Map: ${world.current_map}")
    let prev_map = r/button(panel_x + 10.0, 76.0, 60.0, 22.0, "< Map") != 0
    let next_map = r/button(panel_x + 75.0, 76.0, 60.0, 22.0, "Map >") != 0

    // Tool toggle group — 6 tools must fit in (panel_w - 20) = 240 px.
    r/label(panel_x + 10.0, 104.0, 80.0, 18.0, "Tool:")
    r/toggle_group(panel_x + 10.0, 124.0, 33.0, 24.0,
                   "Sel;Wall;Mon;Sent;Door;Spwn;Gold", world.editor.tool_slot)

    let sx = panel_x + 80.0
    let sw = 150.0
    let mut y = 164.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "speed",   "", ui_bufs.max_speed, 0.0, 1000.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "accel",   "", ui_bufs.accel, 0.0, 10000.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "frict",   "", ui_bufs.friction, 0.0, 10000.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "light r", "", ui_bufs.light_range, 50.0, 2000.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "cone °",  "", ui_bufs.light_half_angle_deg, 1.0, 180.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "lightI",  "", ui_bufs.light_intensity, 0.0, 4.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "ambient", "", ui_bufs.ambient, 0.0, 1.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "cam smth","", ui_bufs.camera_smoothing, 0.0, 30.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "run x",   "", ui_bufs.run_mult, 1.0, 4.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "sneak x", "", ui_bufs.sneak_mult, 0.1, 1.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "stam max","", ui_bufs.stamina_max, 20.0, 200.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "stam drn","", ui_bufs.stamina_drain, 5.0, 100.0); y = y + 22.0
    r/slider(sx, y, sw, 18.0, "stam reg","", ui_bufs.stamina_regen, 5.0, 80.0); y = y + 28.0

    let save_pressed = r/button(panel_x + 10.0, y, 110.0, 28.0, "Save") != 0
    let load_pressed = r/button(panel_x + 130.0, y, 110.0, 28.0, "Load") != 0

    SaveLoadResult {
        save: save_pressed,
        load: load_pressed,
        prev_map: prev_map,
        next_map: next_map,
        new_tuning: read_tuning_from_ui(ui_bufs)
    }
}

⚠️ RESUMABLE EXCEPTION (suspend-depth: 1)
The evaluation is suspended at the throw site — the program waits for a value.
beagle_resume("<expr>") evaluates <expr> and makes it the return value of the throwing expression, so execution continues past the failure. Supply what the failed op should have produced.
While suspended you can beagle_eval to inspect the exact state at the failure point first.
beagle_abort abandons the suspended evaluation.

=> [100.0, 40.0, 25.0]

[main-thread SUSPENDED] SystemError.TypeError { message: "Arithmetic on null: the left operand is null. A null operand usually means a field read returned null — e.g. a field that does not exist on a stale struct instance after a live struct redefinition. Inspect the actual live value.", location: "gameplay/advance_player at /Users/jimmyhmiller/Documents/Code/PlayGround/beagle-zelda/src/gameplay.bg:205" }

The main thread (game loop / GUI) threw and is PAUSED at the throw site — the process is alive.
This is recoverable. Before doing anything, understand the cause: read the error's kind, message, and location above. A "TypeError ... mix integers and floats" whose real cause is a null operand usually means you read a field that does not exist on a value that is still live in the program (a STALE struct instance — see below).

The live data, not your code, is often what's wrong. Changing a struct's shape does NOT migrate instances already alive in the running program: their new fields read as null (or their declared default). So a freshly-constructed value will look fine while the one inside the live world crashes.

To recover, pick the right tool for the cause:
  • Fix the DATA: if state lives in an atom (e.g. a world atom), swap! a migrated value into it, then beagle_main_resume.
  • Resume PAST one bad call: beagle_main_resume("<expr>") evaluates <expr> and uses it as the return value of the throwing expression, so the paused frame continues. Supply the value the failed op should have produced (e.g. a float). Note this only steps past THIS one throw; if the underlying data is still wrong the next frame re-throws — fix the data instead.
  • If the FUNCTION is genuinely buggy: beagle_persist the corrected fn, then beagle_main_resume. But remember the paused frame runs the OLD code — your new fn only takes effect on the NEXT call, so for an in-frame crash you usually also need to resume with a value or fix the data.
  • Give up on this frame: beagle_main_abort.

FIRST inspect the ACTUAL live operands (e.g. eval the real world.player.stamina), never a value you just constructed. If you can't see the cause, say so and ask — do not thrash by editing functions blindly.
